operations.summary: Monitor status of ongoing historical event assembly
operations.notes:
Returns the status of an ongoing historical event assembly, initiated by calling POST historical_events.
The field ?oldest_event_time? shows the last event successfully added to the lifecycle events repository, use this field to track the action's progress.
Events are migrated from newest to oldest, within the specified timeframe.

responseMessages.message: Unauthorized
Access is denied

operations.notes:
Returns a list of ticket lifecycle events in descending order, according to the event timestamp.
Limited to a maximum of five concurrent calls.
By default, This API only returns events that have occurred on R24-1 and later.
If your desired timeframe includes events that occurred in previous versions, you must first run POST historical_events to assemble the data for those events.

parameters.name: get_total
parameters.description: Set to true to get the total number of items that can be retrieved

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Search by type of the event.
parameters.allowableValues: TICKET_CREATION, TICKET_CLOSURE, TICKET_AUTO_CLOSURE_INITIAL_CHECK, TICKET_AUTO_CLOSURE_FROM_SA, TICKET_REJECTED, TICKET_CANCELED, TICKET_RESOLVED, TICKET_REOPENED, TICKET_SLA_OVERDUE, STEP_REDO, STEP_SKIPPED, TASK_DONE, TASK_REASSIGNED, TASK_READY_TO_BE_ASSIGNED, TASK_ASSIGNED, AUTO_STEP_FAILED, TICKET_SLA_STATUS_UPDATE

parameters.name: participant_name
parameters.description: Search by participant users names, Only events of type TASK_ASSIGNED are returned for this filter

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Search for records from this date. Date format expected: yyyy-mm-dd

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Search for records until this date. Date format expected: yyyy-mm-dd

parameters.name: workflow_type
parameters.allowableValues: RULE_MODIFICATION, RULE_RECERTIFICATION, RULE_DECOMMISSION, CLONE_NETWORK_OBJECT_POLICY, DECOMMISSION_NETWORK_OBJECT, ACCESS_REQUEST, MODIFY_GROUP, GENERIC, ACCESS_REQUEST_AND_MODIFY_GROUP

responseMessages.message: ID must be a positive number
Invalid start. should be a positive number
Invalid count. value should be between 1-10000

Invalid type. Permitted types are: [TICKET_CREATION, TICKET_CLOSURE, TICKET_AUTO_CLOSURE_INITIAL_CHECK, TICKET_AUTO_CLOSURE_FROM_SA, TICKET_REJECTED, TICKET_CANCELED, TICKET_RESOLVED, TICKET_REOPENED, TICKET_SLA_OVERDUE, STEP_REDO, STEP_SKIPPED, TASK_DONE, TASK_REASSIGNED, TASK_READY_TO_BE_ASSIGNED, TASK_ASSIGNED, AUTO_STEP_FAILED, TICKET_SLA_STATUS_UPDATE]
Invalid date. Permitted date format is: yyyy-MM-dd (ex. '2020-11-20')
Unauthorized
Access is denied
Invalid character(s): CHARS
Reached max concurrent attempts

operations.summary: Include historical event data in lifecycle queries
operations.notes:
Assembles data from events that occurred before R24-1, allowing these events to be included in GET lifecycle_events.
This action can take some time. Once you call this API, you cannot call it again until the first action has finished.
You can check the action progress by calling GET historical_events_status.
Supported events: [TICKET_CREATION].

responseMessages.message: Accepted
From date is in the future
From date is mandatory
The specified starting date is at max 24 months before earliest lifecycle event time.
Invalid date. Permitted date format is: yyyy-MM-dd (ex. '2020-11-20')
Unauthorized
Access is denied
Already in progress

operations.notes:
Create an asynchronous task to redesign.
Once the Redesign task is created, the status of the task changes to pending and it becomes locked for editing until the redesign completes.
The response location header has a link to an API function which you can use to check the status of the task.
Once the Redesign task is complete, the results become available.

responseMessages.message: Redesign task has been created.
Cannot update ticket when ticket status is not in progress.
You can only update the current step of the ticket. Step ID <ID> does not match the current step ID <ID>.
Task ID <ID> cannot be found in the current step with ID <ID>.
Cannot update ticket when task is not in progress.
The task with ID <ID> does not contain a field with ID <ID>.
Field ID <ID> is not supported in designer.
This action is not permitted because this field does not include designer results.
This action is not permitted because designer is not enabled on the field.
This action is not permitted because designer is not allowed on the field.
Redesign will not be run because it is not needed.
You do not have permission to update this task. Contact your administrator for assistance.
There is no ticket with ID <ID>

operations.summary: Update the device with designer suggestions
operations.notes:
Create an asynchronous task to update Designer suggestions to the specified device.
Once the Update Device task is created, the status of the task changes to pending and it becomes locked for editing until the update completes.
The response location header has a link to an API function which you can use to check the status of the task.
Once the Update Device task is complete, the results become available and are recorded in the ticket's history.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Force update device even if the policy on the device has already been modified

operations.notes:
Maps the rules from the ticket to the relevant rules in SecureTrack.
Adds record set entries to the relevant rules metadata.
Link to history is found in the returned location header.
Applicable only for closed tickets in the following workflows: Access Request, Access Request and Modify Group, and Clone Server Policy.
Use handler_id for running on behalf of another user.
To get handler_id, use the System Users API.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the ticket as shown in SecureChange

parameters.name: handler_id
parameters.description: Run API on behalf of defined user id

responseMessages.message: Started asynchronous mapping of rules to ticket ID <ID>.  Mapping status is displayed in ticket history.

operations.summary: Update the opm device with designer suggestions

Create an asynchronous task to update Designer suggestions to the specified opm device.
Once the Update Device task is created, the status of the task changes to pending and it becomes locked for editing until the update completes.
The response location header has a link to an API function which you can use to check the status of the task.
Once the Update Device task is complete, the results become available and are recorded in the ticket's history.

operations.notes:
Create an asynchronous task to commit policy changes to all the devices managed by the specified management device.
Once the Commit task is created, the task status changes to pending and becomes locked for editing until the task completes.
Use the API function link in the response location header to check the status of the task.
Once the task is complete, the results become available.

assignee_id.description: the internal id of the assignee

participant_id.description: the internal id of the assignee party

participant_name.description: the name of the assignee party

type.allowableValues: TICKET_CREATION, TICKET_CLOSURE, TICKET_AUTO_CLOSURE_INITIAL_CHECK, TICKET_AUTO_CLOSURE_FROM_SA, TICKET_REJECTED, TICKET_CANCELED, TICKET_RESOLVED, TICKET_REOPENED, TICKET_SLA_OVERDUE, STEP_REDO, STEP_SKIPPED, TASK_DONE, TASK_REASSIGNED, TASK_READY_TO_BE_ASSIGNED, TASK_ASSIGNED, AUTO_STEP_FAILED, TICKET_SLA_STATUS_UPDATE

task_id.description: the internal id of the task

workflow_type.allowableValues: RULE_MODIFICATION, RULE_RECERTIFICATION, RULE_DECOMMISSION, CLONE_NETWORK_OBJECT_POLICY, DECOMMISSION_NETWORK_OBJECT, ACCESS_REQUEST, MODIFY_GROUP, GENERIC, ACCESS_REQUEST_AND_MODIFY_GROUP

parent_workflow_id.description: the internal id of the parent workflow

step_id.description: the internal id of the step

assignment_type.description: the type of the assignment. possible types are: assigner, self assigned and reassign group

task_assigned_data.description: the data of the assigned task

potential_participants.description: the potential participants from the workflow

error_tool.allowableValues: SUGGEST_TARGET, DESIGNER, VERIFIER, COMMIT, IMPLEMENTATION, RISK_ANALYSIS

run_status.description: The current state of the history API run

from.description: from date. Date format expected: yyyy-MM-dd

participant_id.description: the internal id of the participant

total.description: Number of items that can be retrieved
